title: Float arithmetic instructions — opcodes 96-102
purpose: The BEAM compiler emits dedicated float arithmetic instructions (fmove, fconv, fadd, fsub, fmul, fdiv, fnegate) for floating-point operations. These use a separate set of float registers (fr0-fr15) that hold raw f64 values, avoiding the overhead of boxing/unboxing Float terms on every operation. Without these 7 opcodes, any Gleam or Erlang code using float arithmetic hits `UnsupportedOpcode`. This blocks math-heavy workflows and any code that the compiler optimizes to use float registers.
task: Add float registers to the Process struct, decode all 7 float opcodes from Generic to dedicated Instruction variants, wire them into dispatch, and implement handlers in a new `opcodes/floats.rs` file.

requirements.title: Float registers in Process
requirements.spec: Add a float register array to the Process struct. BEAM defines 16 float registers (fr0-fr15) holding raw f64 values.
acceptance: Process gains `float_regs: [f64; 16]` field, initialized to 0.0
`Process::get_float_reg(index: u16) -> f64` accessor
`Process::set_float_reg(index: u16, value: f64)` mutator
Index out of bounds (>= 16) returns an error, not a panic
Float registers are independent of X and Y registers
Test: set fr0=3.14, get fr0 returns 3.14

requirements.title: Loader decode — dedicated Instruction variants
requirements.spec: Replace the Generic decode paths for opcodes 96-102 with dedicated Instruction variants. Float register operands use the existing `Operand::FloatRegister(u32)` type (compact.rs:18, decoded at compact.rs:101).
acceptance: `Instruction::Fmove { source, dest }` (opcode 96)
`Instruction::Fconv { source, dest }` (opcode 97)
`Instruction::Fadd { fail, left, right, dest }` (opcode 98)
`Instruction::Fsub { fail, left, right, dest }` (opcode 99)
`Instruction::Fmul { fail, left, right, dest }` (opcode 100)
`Instruction::Fdiv { fail, left, right, dest }` (opcode 101)
`Instruction::Fnegate { fail, source, dest }` (opcode 102)
Float register operands correctly decoded from the compact term format
Existing .beam files with float instructions load without errors

requirements.title: Float opcode handlers
requirements.spec: Implement all 7 float arithmetic handlers in a new `opcodes/floats.rs` file.
acceptance: fmove from x(0) containing boxed Float(3.14) to fr0: fr0 = 3.14
fmove from fr0 to x(0): x(0) = boxed Float(3.14) (heap-allocated)
fconv from x(0) containing integer 42 to fr0: fr0 = 42.0
fdiv(fr0=10.0, fr1=0.0, fr2): badarith error
fnegate(fr0=3.14, fr1): fr1 = -3.14
All arithmetic operations handle NaN and infinity per IEEE 754
Tests cover each opcode with normal and edge cases

requirements.title: Dispatch wiring and module registration
requirements.spec: Wire all 7 float instructions into the dispatch table and register the new floats module.
acceptance: All 7 Instruction variants matched in the dispatch table in mod.rs
`pub mod floats;` added to the opcodes module
No UnsupportedOpcode errors for opcodes 96-102
Test: a compiled module using `X + 1.0` (which compiles to fconv + fadd + fmove) executes correctly

requirements.title: GC awareness for fmove heap allocation
requirements.spec: fmove from a float register to an x/y register allocates a boxed Float on the process heap. This allocation must be GC-safe.
acceptance: fmove to x/y register uses `process.heap_mut().alloc()` + `write_float()`
If heap is full, GC is triggered (same pattern as put_tuple2)
Float registers are NOT part of the GC root set (they hold raw f64, not Term pointers)
Test: fmove after heavy allocation triggers GC and produces correct Float term

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement fclearerror/fcheckerror (different opcodes, separate brief if needed)
SHALL NOT change the Float term boxing format — float registers hold raw f64, conversion to/from boxed Float terms happens in fmove
SHALL NOT implement float comparison instructions — those are separate opcodes
Float registers are process-local (no shared state)
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
